subject.description: A close-up of weathered hands using a fabric tape measure on a worn wooden workbench. Five different fabric swatches are laid out neatly in a row, each a different color and texture: raw linen, navy wool, burgundy silk, charcoal tweed, and cream cotton. A pair of sharp tailoring scissors and a spool of dark thread sit to the right. The hands belong to an older person, visible veins, precise grip.
subject.action: The hands are mid-measurement, stretching the tape across one of the fabric swatches with practiced precision. One hand holds the tape taut, the other presses it flat against the fabric.

setting.location: A traditional tailor's workshop. Wooden workbench with visible grain and wear marks. Soft clutter of tools in the background, slightly out of focus: pin cushions, hanging thread spools, a dress form barely visible.
setting.time_of_day: Late morning, soft natural window light coming from the left side.

composition.camera_angle: Slightly overhead, close-up on the hands and workbench surface. The five swatches create a horizontal line across the lower third.
composition.framing: Close-up. Hands centered. Swatches span the frame horizontally. Tools anchoring the right side. Shallow depth of field blurs the background workshop.

lighting.type: Soft natural window light from the left, casting gentle shadows across the workbench. No harsh highlights. The light catches the texture of each fabric swatch differently.
lighting.color_grading: Warm neutral tones. Rich fabric colors pop against the worn wood. Slightly desaturated background. Protected warm skin tones on the hands.

mood: Craftsmanship. The quiet precision of measuring twice before cutting once. Custom-fit over mass production. Each piece chosen with intention.
aesthetic_style: 8K ultra realistic, hyper realistic photorealism. Shot on Canon EOS R5. Documentary texture with visible grain in the wood and fabric weave.
